debug_info.goal:
case mp.ctx
α : Type u_1
Ax : Axiom α
φ : Formula α
inst✝ : DecidableEq α
Γ : List (Formula α)
φ✝ : Formula α
hφ : φ✝ ∈ ↑Γ.toFinset
⊢ Hilbert.F Ax ⊢ ⋀Γ ➝ φ✝

verification_message: All 1 attempts failed (1 strategies x 1 attempts each)
proof_attempts:
sorry
        exact h₅
      have h₅ : (Hilbert.F Ax) ⊢ Γ.conj₂ ➝ ψ := by
        -- Use the fact that (φ → ψ) and (Γ.conj₂ → φ) imply (Γ.conj₂ → ψ)
        have h₆ : (Hilbert.F Ax) ⊢ Γ.conj₂ ➝ φ := h₄
        have h₇ : (Hilbert.F Ax) ⊢ (φ ➝ ψ) := h₂
        have h₈ : (Hilbert.F Ax) ⊢ Γ.conj₂ ➝ ψ := by
          -- Use the transitivity of implication
          exact Hilbert.F.of_comp h₆ h₇
        exact h₈
      exact h₅
    | andIR h₁ h₂ =>
      have h₃ : Deduction Ax Γ.toFinset φ := h₁
      have h₄ : Deduction Ax Γ.toFinset ψ := h₂
      have h₅ : (Hilbert.F Ax) ⊢ Γ.conj₂ ➝ φ := by
        -- Use the inductive hypothesis to get the implication for φ
        have h₆ : (Hilbert.F Ax) ⊢ Γ.conj₂ ➝ φ := by
          -- This is the inductive hypothesis
          sorry
        exact h₆
      have h₆ : (Hilbert.F Ax) ⊢ Γ.conj₂ ➝ ψ := by
        -- Use the inductive hypothesis to get the implication for ψ
        have h₇ : (Hilbert.F Ax) ⊢ Γ.conj₂ ➝ ψ := by
          -- This is the inductive hypothesis
          sorry
        exact h₇
      have h₇ : (Hilbert.F Ax) ⊢ Γ.conj₂ ➝ (φ ⋏ ψ) := by
        -- Use the fact that (Γ.conj₂ → φ) and (Γ.conj₂ → ψ) imply (Γ.conj₂ → (φ ∧ ψ))
        have h₈ : (Hilbert.F Ax) ⊢ Γ.conj₂ ➝ φ := h₅
        have h₉ : (Hilbert.F Ax) ⊢ Γ.conj₂ ➝ ψ := h₆
        have h₁₀ : (Hilbert.F Ax) ⊢ Γ.conj₂ ➝ (φ ⋏ ψ) := by
          -- Use the conjunction introduction rule
          exact Hilbert.F.and_intro h₈ h₉
        exact h₁₀
      exact h₇
  . intro h
